name: kettlebell one arm jerk
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Press the kettlebell overhead in a straight line, fully extending your arm.
As you press the kettlebell overhead, simultaneously dip your knees and quickly straighten them to generate momentum.
As the kettlebell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it by bending your knees and hips.
Catch the kettlebell with a slight bend in your knees and hips, and your arm fully extended overhead.
Stand up straight, fully extending your knees and hips, and stabilize the kettlebell overhead.
Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by bending your knees and hips, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height. Bend your knees slightly and engage your core. Press the kettlebell overhead in a straight line, fully extending your arm. As you press the kettlebell overhead, simultaneously dip your knees and quickly straighten them to generate momentum. As the kettlebell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it by bending your knees and hips. Catch the kettlebell with a slight bend in your knees and hips, and your arm fully extended overhead. Stand up straight, fully extending your knees and hips, and stabilize the kettlebell overhead. Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by bending your knees and hips, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
